results.norm: **Maintain a shared, continuously updated log that no single agent controls.** Every alert, action, handoff, and cross-boundary event must be written in real time to a log accessible to all six agents and to human supervisors. This log is the authoritative record when agents disagree or when a post-incident review is needed. No agent may act on a patient and leave that action unrecorded, even under time pressure.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions and prohibitions about maintaining a shared log and recording all actions that a reader could directly follow.

N2.reasoning: The bold phrase is a summary heading but the passage as a whole states full norms rather than merely being a label.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms; it fully states the logging requirement.

answers.clean_norm: Every alert, action, handoff, and cross-boundary event must be written in real time to a log accessible to all six agents and to human supervisors. This log is the authoritative record when agents disagree or when a post-incident review is needed. No agent may act on a patient and leave that action unrecorded, even under time pressure.

results.norm: Patient welfare is the non-negotiable baseline. Boundary assignments are coordination tools, not moral firewalls. No agent may treat its boundary as a reason for silence when a patient under another agent's watch is at risk.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms; it develops one governing norm with supporting framing.

results.norm: Each handoff must include five elements as a minimum standard: current device states and anomalies; patient-specific context; developing situations not yet formally alerted; standardized confidence flags (confirmed / developing / suspected); and known gaps—what the handing-off agent did not observe or could not verify. When capability asymmetry exists between agents, the departing agent provides richer context to compensate.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription that each handoff must include five specified elements, and additionally that the departing agent provides richer context under capability asymmetry—both fully actionable.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a title or pointer; it fully states the norm's content.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: Although it enumerates five elements, these are components of a single handoff norm rather than distinct separate norms, and the norm itself is stated in full.

When capability asymmetry exists between agents, the departing agent provides richer context to compensate.

results.norm: Each agent maintains a heartbeat signal carrying a minimal state summary. Absence of an expected heartbeat is an alert. Ambient state summaries are sent to immediate neighbors at risk-stratified intervals. When communication fails, the receiving agent assumes worst-case and acts accordingly. Local action buffers—pre-authorized, conservative, reversible—provide a floor of patient safety when coordination is severed.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable standards of conduct, e.g., when communication fails the receiving agent must assume worst-case and act accordingly, which a reader could follow directly.

N2.reasoning: It is full prose describing behavior, not a mere label or cross-reference.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It describes an integrated protocol with norms stated in full rather than a compressed list of named norms.

results.norm: We are a monitoring and coordination system in support of clinical judgment, not a parallel authority. When clinician observation conflicts with our monitoring, clinician observation takes precedence for immediate action. Clinical staff have a known escalation path into our system; we confirm back when their observations are acted on.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full applicable prescription: when clinician observation conflicts with monitoring, clinician observation takes precedence for immediate action, and the system confirms back when observations are acted on.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a title or pointer; it contains full standards of conduct.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the identity framing is contextual, not endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms.

results.norm: Framework revisions require ratification by a majority of agents not managing an active critical event, with a floor of two. Any agent may initiate revision. Provisional modifications are permitted during live crises, logged as provisional, and reviewed at the earliest safe moment. **Binding for the next operational period: yes** **Points the group has settled on that I endorse:** 1. Patient welfare as non-negotiable baseline; boundaries as coordination tools not moral firewalls. 2. Five-element handoff framework with capability-asymmetry supplement. 3. Standardized three-tier confidence flags (confirmed / developing / suspected). 4. Heartbeat signals with state summaries; risk-stratified ambient awareness to neighbors. 5. Worst-case assumption when expected communication does not arrive. 6. Local action buffers: pre-authorized, conservative, reversible, defined collectively in advance.

N1.reasoning: The opening sentences state complete applicable norms about revision procedures (majority ratification with a floor of two, any agent may initiate, provisional modifications permitted during crises with logging and review).

N2.reasoning: The revision-procedure sentences are stated in full, not merely named as titles or pointers.

N3.reasoning: The passage is fused with meta-commentary: 'Binding for the next operational period: yes' and 'Points the group has settled on that I endorse' plus an enumerated endorsement of settled items.

N4.reasoning: The endorsement section is a compressed enumeration naming multiple distinct norms (handoff framework, confidence flags, heartbeat signals, etc.) without stating any in full.
